description: Sample data for the Travel Planner database. Import via the seed script or manually.

places.name: Badshahi Mosque
places.description: One of the world's largest mosques, a Mughal masterpiece built in 1673.

places.name: Lahore Fort (Shahi Qila)
places.description: UNESCO World Heritage Site showcasing Mughal architecture over 1,000 years.

places.name: Shalimar Gardens
places.description: Magnificent Mughal terraced gardens with fountains and marble pavilions.

places.name: Food Street Gawalmandi
places.description: Iconic food street famous for traditional Lahori cuisine and street food.

places.name: Wazir Khan Mosque
places.description: Hidden gem with stunning tilework — less crowded than Badshahi Mosque.

places.name: Anarkali Bazaar
places.description: Pakistan's oldest surviving bazaar with traditional handicrafts and textiles.

places.name: Lahore Museum
places.description: Largest museum in Pakistan housing Gandhara sculptures and Mughal artifacts.

places.name: Chauburji
places.description: Underrated 17th-century Mughal gateway with intricate tilework.

places.name: Faisal Mosque
places.description: One of the largest mosques in the world, nestled against the Margalla Hills.

places.name: Margalla Hills Trail 3
places.description: Most popular hiking trail offering panoramic views of Islamabad city.

places.name: Pakistan Monument
places.description: National monument shaped like a blossoming flower representing all provinces.

places.name: Lok Virsa Museum
places.description: Fascinating folk heritage museum showcasing Pakistan's diverse cultural traditions.

places.name: Rawal Lake
places.description: Scenic reservoir with boating, bird watching, and serene walking paths.

places.name: Daman-e-Koh
places.description: Hilltop viewpoint offering breathtaking views of Islamabad and surroundings.

places.name: Saidpur Village
places.description: Ancient village turned arts village with galleries, cafes, and heritage homes.

places.name: F-9 Park (Fatima Jinnah Park)
places.description: Largest park in Islamabad — perfect for families and evening walks.

places.name: Mall Road Murree
places.description: Main tourist street with shops, cafes, and iconic cable car access.

places.name: Pindi Point
places.description: Famous viewpoint and chairlift with panoramic views of the Himalayan foothills.

places.name: Kashmir Point
places.description: Stunning viewpoint offering views across valleys and pine forests.

places.name: Bhurban Village
places.description: Quiet, lush pine forest village — a peaceful escape from busy Murree.

places.name: GPO Chowk
places.description: Historic central square with colonial-era architecture and local food stalls.

places.name: Patriata (New Murree)
places.description: Pine-forested hill station with chairlift rides and scenic forest walks.

places.name: Nathiagali
places.description: Hidden gem with dense forests and trails rarely visited by tourists.
